summary:
You are given n nodes labeled 0 to n - 1 and a list of undirected edges, where each edge connects two nodes. Determine whether these nodes and edges form a valid tree.

A graph is a tree when it is fully connected (every node is reachable from every other) and contains no cycles. Equivalently, all n nodes are connected using exactly n - 1 edges.

examples.explanation: All 5 nodes are connected with 4 edges and there is no cycle, so it is a valid tree.
The edges 1-2, 2-3, and 1-3 create a cycle, so the graph is not a tree.

constraints: Each edge connects two distinct nodes in the range 0 to n - 1
Return true only when the graph is connected and acyclic
